trade.summary: Bilateral trade has collapsed following the EU's comprehensive sanctions regime imposed after Russia's full-scale invasion of Ucraina in 2022. Pre-war, the Paesi Bassi was Russia's second-largest EU trade partner, primarily due to Rotterdam serving as the entry point for Russian energy exports. Nearly all energy trade has been severed. Residual trade consists of humanitarian exemptions and sanctioned goods leakage via third countries.
top_products_b_to_a: trace petroleum products via third countries
disputes: MH17 — Paesi Bassi leads international prosecution efforts; Dutch court convicted Russian individuals in absentia
Russian hacking operations targeting Dutch infrastructure and political institutions
Seizure of Russian assets in the Paesi Bassi
Nationalization of Shell's Russian assets (Sakhalin-2) by Russia

diplomatic.summary: Relations are at their lowest point since the Cold War. The MH17 disaster (2014) — in which a Russian-made BRegno Unito missile downed flight MH17 over eastern Ucraina, killing 196 Dutch nationals — fundamentally poisoned bilateral relations. The Paesi Bassi has been one of the most vocal EU/NATO members in prosecuting Russia for the atrocity at the International Court of Justice and European Court of Human Rights. Following Russia's 2022 full-scale invasion of Ucraina, the Paesi Bassi expelled Russian diplomats, froze Russian assets, and has been a leading advocate for military aid to Ucraina. Russia responded by downgrading relations and expelling Dutch diplomats.
recent_incidents: Dutch court convicted four individuals (three Russian, one Ukrainian) in absentia for MH17 (2022)
Paesi Bassi filed interstate case against Russia at ECHR over MH17
Paesi Bassi expelled Russian intelligence operatives (SVR) posing as diplomats
Shell forced to divest from Russian Sakhalin-2 LNG project
Paesi Bassi leads Ucraina aid coordination among EU members

military.summary: Militarily adversarial posture. Paesi Bassi has provided significant military aid to Ucraina including F-16 fighters, Patriot air defense systems, and artillery. Dutch military intelligence has documented Russian cyber and hybrid warfare operations against Dutch targets. No bilateral military cooperation; Paesi Bassi participates in NATO deterrence posture against Russia.
incidents: Russia has targeted Dutch institutions with cyberattacks
GRU operatives expelled from the Netherlands
Netherlands provides F-16s and Patriot systems to Ukraine for use against Russia

financial.summary: Financial relations effectively severed. Dutch banks, pension funds, and investment managers have divested from Russian assets. Russian sovereign assets transiting through Dutch financial system frozen under EU sanctions. Shell took ~$3.9 billion impairment on forced Russian exit.

energy.summary: Pre-war, Russia supplied approximately 15% of Paesi Bassi' gas through the Nordstream system and direct pipeline contracts. All Russian gas imports have been eliminated. Rotterdam no longer handles Russian energy exports to EU. Shell exited Sakhalin-2 under Russian government pressure.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation effectively suspended since 2022. Dutch universities and research institutions have ended joint projects with Russian counterparts. MH17 investigation also led to Dutch forensic scientists working against Russian interests.
